data.title: Lucid (LCID) Stock Sinks despite Key Investor Day Updates
data.description:
Shares of Lucid Group (LCID) are down at the time of writing after the EV company shared several updates during its Investor Day event. One of the biggest announcements was the development of a new mid-size vehicle platform that will support three upcoming electric vehicles. Interestingly, these models are expected to start at around $50,000, which would put them in direct competition with popular vehicles like Tesla's (TSLA) Model 3 and Model Y, as well as other mass-market electric cars.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The first vehicle built on this platform will be a mid-size crossover SUV, followed by a more rugged version inspired by the Gravity X concept, and later, a mid-size sedan. Although these vehicles will be priced lower than Lucid's current Air and Gravity models, the company says that they will still focus on long driving range, efficiency, and spacious interiors. Meanwhile, Lucid expects production of these vehicles to begin in Saudi Arabia around 2026 or 2027, with the company hoping to reach full manufacturing capacity by 2029.

Moreover, at the center of the new platform will be Atlas, which is Lucid's newly designed electric drive unit. According to the company, Atlas uses a smaller, lighter, and simpler design with identical front and rear housings to simplify manufacturing and reduce costs. During the presentation, management also said that Lucid believes it has a path to becoming cash-flow positive later in the decade. In addition, Lucid said that its mid-size platform and planned entry into the robotaxi market could significantly expand the company's overall market opportunity.

Is LCID Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on LCID stock based on zero Buys, six Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average LCID price target of $12.86 per share implies 29.4% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: U.S. Trade Deficit Shrinks as Exports Soar to Record High
data.description:
In January, the U.S. trade deficit fell by 25.3% to $54.5 billion, outpacing the consensus estimate of $66 billion. In addition, exports jumped by 5.5% to an all-time high of $302.1 billion, while imports fell by 0.7% to $356.6 billion. The data was delayed due to last year's government shutdown.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The exports were primarily driven by precious metals amid a surge in gold (XAUUSD) and silver (XAGUSD) prices. During the month, exports of nonmonetary gold and other precious metals rose by $8.8 billion.

U.S. Trade Gap Narrows amid Capital Goods Surge

While imports declined, the U.S. purchased $3.4 billion more of capital goods compared to December, spurred by increased demand for computers and telecommunications equipment. This is likely attributable to the development of AI infrastructure.

Falling imports benefit gross domestic product (GDP) because they reduce the trade deficit and encourage domestic growth. Onshoring production has been a major goal of President Trump, who has used tariffs as a tool to incentivize companies to bring manufacturing back to the U.S.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: "Pretty Privilege" Tests Send Home Depot Stock (NYSE:HD) Slumping
data.description:
Home improvement giant Home Depot (HD) has found itself on the bad end of politics more than once, especially recently with the ongoing issues generated by the Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) branch. But politics may be about to hit Home Depot in a whole new way as a woman decided to test Home Depot's accommodations based on "pretty privilege." The results were unexpected, and investors seemed pretty unhappy about the whole situation. Home Depot shares were down over 1.5% in Thursday morning's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Caroline Ricka, a TikTok content creator, took to Home Depot in two separate outfits. One, as an "off-duty model" in a black dress, and later, dressed more like a "boy" in a sweatshirt and basketball shorts. Her original hypothesis was that she would draw more attention in the dress than in the sweatshirt / shorts combination. This would underscore "pretty privilege," the notion that more attractive people get better treatment in retail.

What Ricka actually found, though, was different. It turned out that more people were talking to her in the "dressed down" look, directly contradicting her hypothesis. Some pointed out that her "dressed down" look seemed more appropriate for someone engaged in home improvement projects, while others pointed out she had tainted the pool to begin with by being followed around by a cameraman.

Give Me Back My Bitcoin

Meanwhile, down in Memphis, Tennessee, a retired man went to buy a simple $10 item, and asked an employee for help in finding it. The man pointed out the item on his phone, but the employee asked to see the phone itself, reports noted. Then, the employee climbed a stepladder to get "better cell service," and upon coming back down, returned the phone.

The man noted that, while the employee was on the stepladder, said employee managed to transfer $500 in Bitcoin (BTC-USD) out of the man's Coinbase (COIN) account, deleting the confirmation emails to cover up the theft. The employee was subsequently fired, and the customer wanted reimbursement. Home Depot reps promised a response, but no response followed. Home Depot claims it is reviewing the matter.

Is Home Depot a Good Long-Term Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on HD stock based on 17 Buys and three Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 0.36% rally in its share price over the past year, the average HD price target of $422.95 per share implies 23.42% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Google (GOOGL) Adds Its Gemini AI Model to Google Maps to Allow More Detailed Questions
data.description:
Tech giant Google (GOOGL) is adding its Gemini artificial intelligence technology to Google Maps through a new feature called "Ask Maps." The new button introduces a chatbot that allows users to ask more detailed questions instead of only searching for directions or locations. For example, someone could ask where they can charge their phone nearby without waiting in line at a café, or find a public tennis court with lights available at night. According to Google Maps executive Miriam Daniel, the goal is to make using maps feel more like having a conversation rather than typing basic search queries.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the same time, the answers generated by Ask Maps will be personalized using a person's past searches and saved trips inside Google Maps. Google says that this update combines the company's constantly updated mapping data with the capabilities of its Gemini AI models. As a result, the company believes it can turn exploring places into a simple conversation while also improving the navigation experience with what it describes as the biggest update to driving tools in more than a decade.

Notably, the Ask Maps feature will begin rolling out on Android and Apple (AAPL) iOS devices in the United States and India, with desktop support coming later. In addition, Google says that the feature will not include advertisements, although it has not ruled out adding them in the future. Historically, Google Maps generates revenue through advertising, promoted business listings, and by charging companies to access its mapping data and APIs. Interestingly, though, analysts say that the service has long been one of Google's more under-monetized products.

Are GOOGL Shares a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on GOOGL stock based on 26 Buys and six Holds assigned in the past three months. Furthermore, the average GOOGL price target of $376.57 per share implies 24.3% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: AbbVie Stock (ABBV) Trades at a Discount Despite Fast Growth — a Bullish Case
data.description:
AbbVie (ABBV) stock trades at a deep discount for a pharma company growing this fast. The market still seems anchored to an old narrative: that AbbVie's best years were tied to its blockbuster drug Humira and that the company's "beat-and-raise" phase is behind it. However, the business that has emerged on the other side of Humira's loss of exclusivity looks stronger and more diversified than investors may realize. In my view, ABBV simply looks too cheap given its strong growth rate and I remain bullish towards the stock.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Even after the increased competition for Humira, the company continues to deliver solid growth, is a powerful cash engine, and offers a dividend yield of around 3%. Yet this large-cap pharmaceutical company's stock is valued more like a mature drugmaker facing major patent cliffs than a company entering the next phase of its growth cycle. It is this mismatch that makes AbbVie interesting.

The Humira Hangover: Why the Market is Mispricing AbbVie

AbbVie currently trades at about 16x forward non-GAAP earnings, a valuation that appears low given its growth outlook. That type of valuation is typically reserved for pharmaceutical companies approaching major patent expirations or facing significant uncertainty in their pipelines. But AbbVie's situation today looks very different. Management expects high single-digit revenue growth through 2029, with earnings growth outpacing sales as operating leverage improves.

Just as important, meaningful patent risk appears limited this decade. Only about 9% of AbbVie's revenue faces loss of exclusivity through 2030, and key assets have protection extending well beyond that.

What makes the current valuation even more surprising is that AbbVie traded at similar multiples several years ago when growth was slower, and the Humira patent cliff was still looming. Today, the business is cleaner, growth visibility is better, and the product mix is stronger, yet the market has not fully adjusted.

Skyrizi and Rinvoq Are Still Early in Their Growth Curve

A big part of the market's skepticism revolves around AbbVie's immunology franchise. Some investors believe the growth driven by Skyrizi, a leader in psoriasis treatment, and Rinvoq, a popular rheumatoid arthritis drug, is nearing its peak. But that view may underestimate how much runway remains for these drugs.

Skyrizi and Rinvoq continue expanding into new indications and patient populations, particularly in inflammatory bowel disease and dermatology. Importantly, the overall immunology market continues to expand, allowing AbbVie to benefit not only from market share but also from a growing treated population.

Recent clinical results reinforce the above opportunity. In a Phase 3 study for Crohn's disease, Skyrizi achieved clinical remission in 55% of patients at week 12, compared with 30% on placebo, with even stronger outcomes among treatment-naive patients. These results help strengthen the drug's positioning in a large and growing market. The bigger point is that AbbVie's immunology franchise is no longer simply replacing Humira revenue. It is becoming a long-term growth engine in its own right.

The Business Is More Diversified Than Investors Think

Another reason AbbVie may look undervalued is that many investors still view it primarily as an immunology company. In reality, the business has become much more diversified. Neuroscience, oncology, and aesthetics are all contributing meaningful revenue streams, helping reduce reliance on any single therapeutic category.

Botox therapeutics and aesthetics remain major contributors, and neuroscience continues to expand through new drug development programs. AbbVie is also advancing several pipeline candidates in areas such as Parkinson's disease, psychiatric disorders, and metabolic treatments.

This broader portfolio matters because it gives AbbVie multiple paths to growth rather than depending on one or two blockbuster drugs. For a company of this size, diversification can make future revenue streams far more durable.

Cash Flow Strength Supports the Bull Case

AbbVie's valuation also appears disconnected from its underlying cash generation. In 2025, the company generated nearly $18 billion in free cash flow, easily covering the $11.7 billion it paid out in dividends. That strong cash generation allows AbbVie to maintain an attractive dividend while continuing to invest heavily in research and development.

The company's dividend yield currently sits around 3%, and AbbVie has more than quadrupled its payout since becoming an independent company in 2013. Beyond dividends, AbbVie also maintains the flexibility to pursue acquisitions or partnerships that could strengthen its long-term growth profile. The company may still have up to $15 billion in potential deal capacity, giving management room to add pipeline assets without relying on a single transformative acquisition.

That combination—growth, income, and strategic flexibility—is relatively rare among large pharmaceutical companies.

What the Market May Be Missing

The market often rewards companies when growth surprises investors. But once expectations begin to stabilize, valuations can lag even when the underlying business remains strong. That appears to be happening with AbbVie today.

Investors may be interpreting a normalization of earnings surprises as evidence that growth is slowing. In reality, the company may simply be entering a phase of more predictable expansion driven by multiple products rather than a single blockbuster.

For a large pharmaceutical company with limited near-term patent risk, strong free cash flow, and visible growth drivers, a valuation multiple below 16x earnings looks difficult to justify.

Wall Street's View

According to TipRanks, AbbVie carries a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating, with 15 Buy, seven Hold, and zero Sell ratings. Based on 22 Wall Street analysts, the average 12-month price target is $253.47, implying roughly 11.3% upside from the recent price of $227.68.
data.author: Davit Kirakosyan

data.title: Why SoundHound AI Stock (SOUN) Is Falling Today, 03/12/26?
data.description:
Shares of SoundHound AI (SOUN) fell nearly 3% at the market open on Thursday. With no company-specific news driving the move, the decline appears tied to a broader market sell-off as volatility rises amid surging oil prices and escalating tensions involving Iran in the Middle East. This led to investor uncertainty and triggered declines across several technology and growth stocks. Overall, SOUN stock is down over 5% in the last five trading days.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, SoundHound AI focuses on voice recognition and natural language processing, providing AI-powered solutions for multiple industries.

Why SoundHound's Strong Results Haven't Lifted the Stock

Beyond the broader market pressures, the recent decline in SOUN may also reflect a muted investor reaction to its earnings report released late last month.

Demand for voice AI helped SoundHound AI deliver strong revenue growth. In 2025, the company delivered record revenue of $169 million, almost doubling the $84.7 million recorded the year before. Yet the stock hasn't followed the same upward path. Investors are still questioning when the company will become profitable, how it will compete in a crowded AI market, and whether its rapid expansion can continue. Until those concerns ease, strong revenue growth alone may not be enough to lift the stock.

Growth at SoundHound AI remains strong, but the pace has slowed lately. Fast-growing companies usually report accelerating quarterly growth, yet that trend hasn't been evident. After a sharp jump earlier, the company's growth rate has cooled, pointing to a more normal pace of expansion.

Additionally, investors could be concerned by SoundHound's continued losses. For the full year 2025, adjusted EBITDA was a loss of $58.4 million. Overall, lack of profitability, ongoing cash burn, and continued acquisitions make the stock a risky prospect.

Is SOUN a Good Stock to Buy?

According to TipRanks, SOUN stock has received a Strong Buy consensus rating, with five Buys and one Hold assigned in the last three months. The average SoundHound stock price target is $14.80, suggesting a potential upside of 92% from the current level.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Palantir Stock (PLTR) Rises on New AI Partnerships with GE, NVIDIA, Centrus, Ondas
data.description:
At its AIPCon 9 conference, Palantir Technologies (PLTR) announced several major partnerships with GE Aerospace (GE), Ondas (ONDS), NVIDIA (NVDA), Centrus Energy (LEU), and LG CNS. These announcements focus on integrating Palantir's Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP) across defense, energy, manufacturing, and AI infrastructure sectors. Following the news, PLTR stock was up 2% on Thursday.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Deals Show PLTR's Growing Role in AI Operations

Here are the highlights from the new partnerships:

NVIDIA: Palantir and NVIDIA are teaming up to deliver "sovereign AI operating system reference architecture". This brings together NVIDIA's Blackwell Ultra systems with Palantir's AIP to provide a complete, production-ready AI data center solution that lets organizations keep full control over their data, models, and applications.

GE Aerospace: Expanding their existing partnership, GE Aerospace and Palantir are using "agentic AI" to enhance military aircraft readiness for the U.S. Air Force, specifically focusing on the J85 engine that powers the T-38 training fleet. This includes optimizing supply chains, manufacturing, and maintenance.

Ondas Holdings: Palantir partnered with Ondas and World View to build a "next-generation multi-domain intelligence platform". The partnership combines Ondas's autonomous drone systems and World View's high-altitude stratospheric balloons with Palantir AIP to boost intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance (ISR) for defense customers.

Centrus Energy: Centrus is partnering with Palantir to drive cost savings and operational efficiencies as it expands U.S. uranium enrichment capacity.

LG CNS: LG CNS and PLTR expanded their partnership to accelerate AI transformation across the LG Group. This formalizes their plan to bring stronger, AI‑driven business impact across LG's wide range of companies.

Overall, these deals indicate that the companies are turning to Palantir to bring their data together, scale AI, and modernize critical operations. From aircraft and drones to nuclear fuel, AI infrastructure, and manufacturing, Palantir's software is becoming a core tool for companies moving toward digital and AI‑driven operations.

Is Palantir a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Overall, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Palantir based on 14 Buy, four Hold, and two Sell recommendations. The average PLTR stock price target of $191.76 indicates 24.89% upside potential.
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Down Today, 3/12/26?
data.description:
March has been a volatile month for the stock market, and more pain could be on the way as investors digest a new tariff update and developments from the U.S.-Iran war.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In another update to the tariff saga, the U.S. initiated Section 301 trade investigations into 16 of its trading partners, including China, the European Union, Mexico, and India. The investigations hinge on excess capacity and production, and could result in tariffs or fees if the U.S. concludes that the countries engaged in unfair trade practices. Separately, U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said that the U.S. will conduct additional Section 301 investigations targeting 60 countries as early as Thursday.

U.S.-Iran War Elevates Energy Prices

Meanwhile, ship traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remains at a near standstill as oil and gas prices soar. In his first publicly broadcast message as Iran's new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei said that the passage will remain closed as a "tool to pressure the enemy" and emphasized that U.S. bases in the Middle East will be attacked.

On Thursday, U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright stated that the Navy could begin escorting tankers through the strait by the end of the month. Earlier this week, Wright posted on X that the U.S. had successfully escorted a tanker through the shipping lane before quickly deleting the post.
data.author: Eddie Pan
